The Financial Reality

The Industrial Brain Drain

A veteran technician retirement wave is hitting simultaneously across the US, Europe, and Asia. When they leave, decades of tacit diagnostic intuition — the exact sound of a failing bearing, the quirky vibration of a misaligned pump — leaves with them.

Existing CMMS tools (MaintainX, UpKeep, Fiix) are excellent for scheduling, but they throw away the diagnostic reasoning of why a fix worked once a ticket closes.

A 10% improvement in technician retention saves ~1.5 salaries per plant annually.

The cost of lost knowledge compounding across a network is catastrophic to uptime.

TICKET: PUMP-402 (CLOSED)

"Swapped inboard bearings. Good to go."

Critical Context Lost
  • What did it sound like before failure?
  • What was the exact vibration signature?
  • Why these specific bearings over standard?
MNEMAI FAILURE FINGERPRINT: PUMP-402
Voice Transcript Captured

"Getting a high-pitched squeal at load, vibration in zone 3. Swapped inboard bearings for high-temp variants."

Extracted Symptom
High-pitch squeal + Z3 Vib
Structured Fix
High-temp inboard bearings
Searchable & Matchable Network-Wide
How It Works

The Operational Memory Loop

A continuously compounding cycle that turns unstructured field notes into structured network intelligence.

1 Capture
2 Structure
3 Match
4 Compound

Capture at the Edge

Technicians log low-friction voice notes, forms, or auto-suggested tags at the exact point of repair using a mobile app. No heavy typing required.

  • → Voice-to-text with industrial acoustic filtering
  • → Pulls sensor telemetry at the exact timestamp
  • → Works in offline/low-connectivity environments

Structure with AI

MnemAI uses specialized LLMs to extract a structured 'failure fingerprint' — Symptom → Cause → Fix — from messy, jargon-heavy technician language.

  • → Normalizes slang across different plants
  • → Eliminates tedious 15-field manual data entry
  • → Maps to ISO 14224 maintenance standards automatically
> Processing raw input...
Entity: Pump 402
Symptom: High frequency vibration
Resolution: Impeller alignment

Real-time Matching

When new anomalies occur, they are checked against the structured fingerprint library. MnemAI surfaces confidence-ranked diagnostic suggestions to the active technician.

  • → Reduces Mean Time To Diagnose (MTTD)
  • → Surfaces past solutions for identical equipment classes
  • → Up-skills junior technicians immediately

Federated Compounding

Every resolved event makes the model smarter. Through Federated Learning, your entire plant network shares pattern intelligence without sharing raw data.

  • → Plant A's fix becomes Plant B's suggestion
  • → Raw proprietary data never leaves your environment
  • → The knowledge base scales with your operations
Beta Goals

Targeting concrete operational improvements across the manufacturing floor.

AI Technology & Infrastructure

Built on enterprise AWS architecture.

MnemAI leverages state-of-the-art multimodal extraction and time-series similarity models. Our infrastructure is designed for high-throughput sensor environments and strict data isolation.

Amazon Bedrock & LLMs

Powers our conversational interface and extraction engine. Transforms unstructured technician speech into rigid, searchable taxonomies (ISO 14224 compliant) instantly.

Amazon SageMaker

Runs our GPU-backed time-series anomaly detection. Matches current vibration, acoustic, and pressure signatures against historical 'failure fingerprints' to rank confidence.

IoT Core & Timestream

Ingests and stores high-throughput sensor telemetry. Syncs in real-time with existing CMMS platforms (MaintainX, Fiix) via AWS Lambda and API Gateway.

S3 & Strict Isolation

Client raw data is strictly isolated via IAM policies. The federated learning model shares only mathematical weights, never proprietary operational specifications.

Why Now

A converging crisis and capability.

The industrial world is facing a demographic cliff. As veteran technicians retire, they take decades of undocumented, highly specific operational memory with them. At the exact same moment, multimodal AI and time-series similarity models have reached the maturity required to understand messy, unstructured shop-floor speech and translate it into rigid data structures. What was impossible three years ago is now imperative.

See why the timing matters →
Market Strategy

The Industrial Memory Market

Targeting maintenance-heavy sectors where downtime costs millions per hour.

Target Users

Maintenance-heavy industrial plants across manufacturing, energy, and utilities. Environments where diagnostic reasoning—not just part replacement—is the bottleneck to uptime.

Go-to-Market

Direct enterprise pilot sales to plant operations and reliability teams. Starting with 90-day design partnerships to validate failure-fingerprint extraction on real floor data.

Revenue Model

Annual recurring revenue based on a per-seat technician subscription, plus scaled tiers based on asset-class complexity and API integration needs.

Current Status

Enterprise Pilot Program Opening 2026.

We are currently in active development on our core extraction engine, fine-tuning our acoustic models against real-world shop floor noise profiles. We are selectively onboarding design partners to participate in closed beta testing starting next year.

Product Roadmap

The Path to Predictive Memory

A phased approach to building a compounding industrial intelligence network.

PHASE 0

Pilot Integration

Deploy mobile voice-capture in isolated test environments. Validate symptom-to-fix extraction accuracy against noisy background audio.

PHASE 1

Failure-Fingerprint Library

Establish the foundational taxonomy. Map unstructured technician notes to ISO 14224 standards and begin localized confidence ranking for suggested fixes.

PHASE 2

Federated Cross-Plant Matching

Enable encrypted weight sharing. Allow Plant A's resolved anomalies to instantly inform diagnostic suggestions at Plant B without exposing proprietary operational data.

PHASE 3

Predictive Alerts

Integrate continuous real-time telemetry from AWS IoT Core. Surface proactive alerts based on historical failure fingerprints before a hard fault occurs.

PHASE 4

Multi-Industry Expansion

Generalize the matching engine beyond primary manufacturing into energy grids, maritime operations, and broad heavy infrastructure.
